missions.title: Junior Linux Administrator
missions.description: You've just joined a startup as a Linux sysadmin. The servers are under load and the team needs answers fast.

stages.title: Server Health Check
stages.objective: Count how many ERROR lines are in server.log
stages.solution: grep -c 'ERROR' server.log
stages.hint: Use grep -c to count matching lines

stages.title: Identify Top Errors
stages.objective: List unique error messages and their frequency
stages.solution: grep 'ERROR' server.log | awk '{print $NF}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn
stages.hint: Extract last field, sort, count, re-sort

stages.title: Timeline the Incident
stages.objective: Show errors grouped by hour of day
stages.solution: grep 'ERROR' server.log | awk '{print $2}' | cut -d: -f1 | sort | uniq -c
stages.hint: Extract timestamp field, get hour component

stages.title: Generate Report
stages.objective: Show total errors and top 3 unique error types
stages.solution: echo "Total: $(grep -c ERROR server.log)"; grep ERROR server.log | awk '{print $NF}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -3
stages.hint: Use command substitution $() to embed results

missions.title: Web Operations Engineer
missions.description: The e-commerce site is slow and users are complaining. Investigate the Apache logs to find what's wrong.

stages.title: Traffic Overview
stages.objective: Show total request count and unique visitor count from apache.log
stages.solution: echo "Requests: $(wc -l < apache.log)"; echo "Unique IPs: $(awk '{print $1}' apache.log | sort -u | wc -l)"
stages.hint: wc -l for total, then extract IPs, deduplicate, count

stages.title: Error Rate
stages.objective: Calculate the percentage of 5xx errors in total traffic
stages.solution: awk '{t++; if($9>=500) e++} END {printf "Error rate: %.1f%%\n", e/t*100}' apache.log
stages.hint: Count total and 500+ errors, compute ratio

stages.title: Top Endpoints
stages.objective: Find the 5 most requested URL paths
stages.solution: awk '{print $7}' apache.log | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -5
stages.hint: Field 7 is the URL path

stages.title: Bot Detection
stages.objective: List IPs making more than 50 requests (potential bots)
stages.solution: awk '{c[$1]++} END {for(ip in c) if(c[ip]>50) print c[ip], ip}' apache.log | sort -rn
stages.hint: Count per IP in awk array, filter in END block

stages.title: Incident Report
stages.objective: Show 404 error count and top 3 missing URLs
stages.solution: echo "404s: $(awk '$9==404' apache.log | wc -l)"; awk '$9==404 {print $7}' apache.log | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -3
stages.hint: Filter on field 9 == 404, then count URLs

missions.title: Physical Design Engineer
missions.description: The chip is failing timing closure. Analyze EDA reports to find violations and generate a fix-priority list.

stages.title: Violation Count
stages.objective: Count total timing violations in timing.rpt
stages.solution: grep -c 'VIOLATED' timing.rpt
stages.hint: grep -c counts matching lines directly

stages.title: Worst Paths
stages.objective: List the 5 worst violated paths (most negative slack)
stages.solution: grep 'VIOLATED' timing.rpt | sort -t'|' -k3 -n | head -5
stages.hint: Sort numerically on the slack column

stages.title: Clock Inventory
stages.objective: List all clock names defined in constraints.sdc
stages.solution: grep 'create_clock' constraints.sdc | grep -oE '\-name [^ ]+' | awk '{print $2}'
stages.hint: Extract -name argument from create_clock lines

stages.title: Library Census
stages.objective: Count total standard cells in liberty.lib
stages.solution: grep -c 'cell (' liberty.lib
stages.hint: Count lines matching the cell definition pattern

stages.title: Synthesis Summary
stages.objective: Extract area and timing information from synthesis.log
stages.solution: grep -E 'Area|Timing|Slack|Frequency' synthesis.log | head -20
stages.hint: Search for key metric keywords in the synthesis report

missions.title: HR Data Analyst
missions.description: The HR director needs a full workforce report before the board meeting. You have 10 minutes and only a terminal.

stages.title: Headcount
stages.objective: Count total number of employees (excluding header)
stages.solution: tail -n +2 employees.csv | wc -l
stages.hint: Skip header with tail -n +2, then count

stages.title: Department Breakdown
stages.objective: Show employee count per department, sorted largest first
stages.solution: awk -F',' 'NR>1 {print $3}' employees.csv | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn
stages.hint: Column 3 is department. Use NR>1 to skip header.

stages.title: Salary Statistics
stages.objective: Calculate min, max, and average salary
stages.solution: awk -F',' 'NR>1 {s+=$4; if(min==""||$4<min)min=$4; if($4>max)max=$4} END {printf "Min: %s  Max: %s  Avg: %.0f\n", min, max, s/(NR-1)}' employees.csv
stages.hint: Track running min, max, and sum. Divide sum by count in END.

stages.title: High Earners
stages.objective: List employees earning over $100,000, sorted by salary descending
stages.solution: awk -F',' 'NR>1 && $4>100000 {print $4, $2, $3}' employees.csv | sort -rn
stages.hint: Filter where field 4 > 100000, then sort

missions.title: Security Analyst
missions.description: The security team suspects a brute-force attack and data exfiltration attempt. Investigate the logs.

stages.title: Auth Failures
stages.objective: Count authentication failures in syslog.log
stages.solution: grep -ic 'error\|fail\|denied' syslog.log
stages.hint: Use grep -ic for case-insensitive count of failure keywords

stages.title: Suspicious IPs
stages.objective: Find IPs with more than 20 requests in apache.log
stages.solution: awk '{c[$1]++} END {for(ip in c) if(c[ip]>20) print c[ip], ip}' apache.log | sort -rn
stages.hint: Count per IP, filter those over threshold

stages.title: Error Pattern
stages.objective: Show hourly distribution of 4xx and 5xx errors from apache.log
stages.solution: awk '$9>=400 {print $4}' apache.log | grep -oE '[0-9]+:[0-9]+' | cut -d: -f1 | sort | uniq -c
stages.hint: Filter errors, extract hour from timestamp

stages.title: Exfil Detection
stages.objective: Find the largest response sizes in apache.log (field 10) — data exfiltration sends large responses
stages.solution: awk '{print $10, $1, $7}' apache.log | sort -rn | head -10
stages.hint: Field 10 is bytes sent. Sort descending to find largest.

missions.title: Pipeline Architect
missions.description: Build a complete monitoring dashboard using only command-line pipes. Each stage adds a new metric.

stages.title: Traffic Metrics
stages.objective: In one pipeline: total requests, unique IPs, and peak hour from apache.log
stages.solution: echo "Requests: $(wc -l < apache.log)"; echo "IPs: $(awk '{print $1}' apache.log | sort -u | wc -l)"; echo "Peak hour: $(awk '{print $4}' apache.log | grep -oE '[0-9]+:[0-9]+' | cut -d: -f1 | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -1)"
stages.hint: Use command substitution $() for each metric

stages.title: Error Dashboard
stages.objective: Show count of each HTTP error code (400+) sorted by frequency
stages.solution: awk '$9>=400 {print $9}' apache.log | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn
stages.hint: Filter on field 9 >= 400, count each distinct code

stages.title: Bandwidth Report
stages.objective: Calculate total MB transferred and average MB per request
stages.solution: awk '{t+=$10; n++} END {printf "Total: %.1fMB  Avg: %.3fMB\n", t/1048576, t/n/1048576}' apache.log
stages.hint: Sum field 10 (bytes), divide by 1048576 for MB

stages.title: URL Performance
stages.objective: Top 5 URLs and their total bytes served
stages.solution: awk '{bytes[$7]+=$10} END {for(u in bytes) print bytes[u], u}' apache.log | sort -rn | head -5
stages.hint: Use awk array indexed by URL, accumulate bytes

stages.title: Complete Dashboard
stages.objective: Generate a formatted summary: header, metrics, error table, top 3 URLs
stages.solution:
printf '=== Server Dashboard ===
'; printf 'Requests: %s
' $(wc -l < apache.log); awk '$9>=400{e[$9]++} END{print "
--- Errors ---"; for(c in e) printf "%s: %d
", c, e[c]}' apache.log | sort; echo; echo '--- Top URLs ---'; awk '{c[$7]++} END {for(u in c) print c[u], u}' apache.log | sort -rn | head -3
stages.hint: Combine all previous steps with printf for formatting
